{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Chief Operating Officer (Oil Palm)",
  "description": "Bridgemead HR provides customized HR solutions to optimize workforce productivity and profitability. Our expert trainers design bespoke training programs, innovative HR strategies, and efficient payroll systems tailored to each client&rsquo;s unique needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Operating Officer (Oil Palm)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	Provide strategic leadership and oversight of all operational functions across plantation, processing, and distribution.
	The COO drives operational efficiency, productivity, and profitability while ensuring alignment with the company&rsquo;s long - term growth objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily operations across plantation, milling, refining, and logistics.
	Develop and implement operational strategies aligned with business goals.
	Drive cost optimization and operational efficiency across all units.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, environmental, and regulatory standards.
	Monitor production performance and implement continuous improvement initiatives.
	Collaborate with finance to manage budgets, forecasts, and cost controls.
	Lead and supervise senior operational managers and departmental heads.
	Optimize supply chain and distribution processes.
	Establish and track operational KPIs and performance metrics.
	Support business expansion, investments, and strategic initiatives.


Requirements / Qualifications
Educational Qualifications:


	MSc, BSc or HND in Agriculture, Engineering, Business Administration, or related field


Professional Certifications:


	MBA or relevant executive management certification (preferred)


Experience Requirement:


	Minimum of 15+ years of experience in operations management, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role within agribusiness or manufacturing.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: career.bridgemeadnghr@gmail.com using &quot;Chief Operating Officer&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Chief Operating Officer (Oil Palm) Job at Bridgemead HR. and Advisory




        
    

        
        Bridgemead HR provides customized HR solutions to optimize workforce productivity and profitability. Our expert trainers design bespoke training programs, innovative HR strategies, and efficient payroll systems tailored to each client’s unique needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Operating Officer (Oil Palm)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

            
    




	Provide strategic leadership and oversight of all operational functions across plantation, processing, and distribution.
	The COO drives operational efficiency, productivity, and profitability while ensuring alignment with the company’s long - term growth objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily operations across plantation, milling, refining, and logistics.
	Develop and implement operational strategies aligned with business goals.
	Drive cost optimization and operational efficiency across all units.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, environmental, and regulatory standards.
	Monitor production performance and implement continuous improvement initiatives.
	Collaborate with finance to manage budgets, forecasts, and cost controls.
	Lead and supervise senior operational managers and departmental heads.
	Optimize supply chain and distribution processes.
	Establish and track operational KPIs and performance metrics.
	Support business expansion, investments, and strategic initiatives.


Requirements / Qualifications
Educational Qualifications:


	MSc, BSc or HND in Agriculture, Engineering, Business Administration, or related field


Professional Certifications:


	MBA or relevant executive management certification (preferred)


Experience Requirement:


	Minimum of 15+ years of experience in operations management, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role within agribusiness or manufacturing.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Chief Operating Officer" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Front Office / Customer Service Representative",
  "description": "BamBau Cafe, a restaurant that serves the best meals, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Office / Customer Service Representative

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	A restaurant in Lekki is seeking a friendly, professional, and customer-focused Front Office/Customer Service Representative to serve as the first point of contact for customers.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for welcoming guests, taking and processing orders, handling customer inquiries, and managing online customer interactions to ensure an exceptional customer experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to customers promptly and professionally upon arrival.
	Assist customers with menu selections and provide information on products, meals, and promotions.
	Take customer orders accurately and relay them to the kitchen team.
	Process payments and issue receipts where applicable.
	Handle customer inquiries, complaints, and feedback professionally and efficiently.
	Respond to customer messages, inquiries, and orders received through social media, WhatsApp, phone calls, and other online channels.
	Coordinate with the kitchen and delivery teams to ensure timely order fulfillment.
	Maintain a clean, organized, and welcoming front-of-house environment.
	Monitor customer satisfaction and escalate issues where necessary.
	Support daily administrative and operational activities as assigned.


Requirements


	Minimum of National Diploma (ND) in Business Administration, Mass Communication, Hospitality Management, or a related field.
	Minimum of 1&ndash;2 years of experience in customer service, front desk, reception, or a customer-facing role.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Strong interpersonal and relationship management skills.
	Proficiency in the use of WhatsApp Business, social media platforms, and basic computer applications.
	Ability to multitask and work efficiently in a fast-paced environment.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy in order-taking and record-keeping.
	Professional appearance and positive attitude.
	Experience in the hospitality, restaurant, or food service industry is an added advantage.


Salary range
N100,000 - N150,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: sixwings.ng@gmail.com using the Job Position as subject of the mail.",

Front Office / Customer Service Representative Job at BamBau Cafe




        
    

        
        BamBau Cafe, a restaurant that serves the best meals, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Office / Customer Service Representative

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

            
    




	A restaurant in Lekki is seeking a friendly, professional, and customer-focused Front Office/Customer Service Representative to serve as the first point of contact for customers.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for welcoming guests, taking and processing orders, handling customer inquiries, and managing online customer interactions to ensure an exceptional customer experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to customers promptly and professionally upon arrival.
	Assist customers with menu selections and provide information on products, meals, and promotions.
	Take customer orders accurately and relay them to the kitchen team.
	Process payments and issue receipts where applicable.
	Handle customer inquiries, complaints, and feedback professionally and efficiently.
	Respond to customer messages, inquiries, and orders received through social media, WhatsApp, phone calls, and other online channels.
	Coordinate with the kitchen and delivery teams to ensure timely order fulfillment.
	Maintain a clean, organized, and welcoming front-of-house environment.
	Monitor customer satisfaction and escalate issues where necessary.
	Support daily administrative and operational activities as assigned.


Requirements


	Minimum of National Diploma (ND) in Business Administration, Mass Communication, Hospitality Management, or a related field.
	Minimum of 1–2 years of experience in customer service, front desk, reception, or a customer-facing role.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Strong interpersonal and relationship management skills.
	Proficiency in the use of WhatsApp Business, social media platforms, and basic computer applications.
	Ability to multitask and work efficiently in a fast-paced environment.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy in order-taking and record-keeping.
	Professional appearance and positive attitude.
	Experience in the hospitality, restaurant, or food service industry is an added advantage.


Salary range
N100,000 - N150,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the mail.

Executive Assistant to the Managing Director Job at Kolomoni Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Kolomoni Microfinance Bank is a fast-growing financial institution committed to providing innovative financial solutions that empower individuals and businesses.

As we continue our growth and transformation journey, we are seeking an individual to provide high-level support to the Managing Director in the capacity below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant to the Managing Director

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Department: Office of the Managing Director
Reports To: Managing Director

The Role
     

            
    




	The Executive Assistant will serve as the primary point of coordination for the Managing Director's office, ensuring efficient day-to-day operations, seamless stakeholder management, and effective execution of strategic priorities.
	This role requires a highly organized, proactive, and discreet professional who can manage multiple priorities while maintaining the highest standards of professionalism and confidentiality.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the Managing Director's calendar, meetings, and appointments.
	Coordinate executive meetings, board sessions, and stakeholder engagements.
	Prepare meeting agendas, reports, presentations, and briefing materials.
	Draft and manage correspondence on behalf of the Managing Director.
	Track action items and ensure timely follow-up across departments.
	Conduct research and prepare executive summaries on business and industry matters.
	Coordinate travel, accommodation, and logistics arrangements.
	Manage executive-level relationships with regulators, partners, vendors, and other stakeholders.
	Maintain confidential records, files, and documentation.
	Support strategic initiatives and special projects as assigned.


Requirements
Qualifications:


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Management, English, Law, or a related discipline.
	Professional certifications such as CIPM, ICSAN, or related qualifications will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in an Executive Assistant, Personal Assistant, Office Management, or similar role.
	Experience supporting senior executives or C-suite leaders is strongly preferred.
	Previous experience within banking, financial services, or other regulated industries will be an advantage.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Strong organizational and time management skills.
	High level of discretion and confidentiality.
	Exceptional attention to detail.
	Strong stakeholder management and interpersonal skills.
	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Ability to work independently and under pressure in a fast-paced environment.
	Strong presentation development and report-writing skills.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and benefits package.
	HMO coverage.
	Professional development opportunities.
	Performance-based rewards.
	Opportunity to work closely with senior leadership and contribute to organizational growth.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and a cover letter to: [email protected] using "Application – Executive Assistant to the Managing Director" as the subject of the mail.

Note: Kolomoni Microfinance Bank is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Budget & Financial Analyst",
  "description": "Egbin Power Plc is one of the biggest additions to the electricity industry in Nigeria. Often seen as the biggest single generating power station in Sub-Saharan Africa, it was built to meet the ever-rising demand for electricity in Lagos and its environs.

Following the nation&rsquo;s privatization exercise, Sahara, through its power division, Sahara Power Group and sundry affiliations, acquired the 1320 MW installed capacity Egbin Power Plant. We are working towards deploying a minimum of 5,000 MW of electricity generation over the next five years.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Budget &amp; Financial Analyst

Job Identification: 544
Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria.
Job Schedule: Full time
Job Category: Non-Technical

Job Description


	The Budget &amp; Financial Analyst assists in managing Egbin&rsquo;s budget preparation and monitoring process, including the preparation of relevant reports to support Management&rsquo;s decision-making.


Key Duties and Responsibilities


	Assist in circulating the annual business planning guidelines and provide assistance to other budget holders during the planning process in conjunction with the performance and planning unit.
	Develop a consolidated budget pack for review and approval by the Management Account &amp; Budget Manager.
	Monitor approved budget allocations, revenue, and expenditure and highlight variances for the attention of the Head, Management Account &amp; Budget.
	Carry out primary review and validation of budget data presented by various units and or highlight major inconsistencies/shortcomings.
	Carry out initial review &amp; analysis for consistency of data, validation with other operational information and present to the head of the section.
	Ensure the reporting of the monthly financial performance of each unit and business generating centres (BGCs)
	Carry out variance analysis of major performance parameters with that of budget forecasts, and highlight underlying business/operational causes of variation&nbsp;
	Carry out studies and research of relevant authoritative sources of macroeconomic data to gather basic information on budgetary assumptions.
	Prepare first drafts of relevant periodic and/ or ad hoc reports.
	Assist in generating monthly management accounts, business performance statements, noting major deviations from budget, and risk areas.
	Participate in the analysis of operational results and proffer recommendations to management.
	Obtain key insights on industry (competitors) and economic analysis, and recommend areas of improvement (If any).
	Provide analytic insight using trend analysis on key operational performance and accounting indices.
	Prepares draft financial forecast to aid business decision-making.
	Prepare monthly financial investment analysis and suggest best alternatives for fund based on economic performance.
	Perform any other duties as may be assigned by the Head of Budget, Planning and Business Performance.
	Implement data protection policies and practices of Egbin Power Plc.
	Ensure compliance with the NDPR and other data protection laws, and data protection policies.
	Undertake Data Protection Impact Assessment as applicable to curb the risk during data processing operations.
	Ensure awareness, understanding and application of QHSE policy and application of departmental objectives.
	Ensure awareness of the Integrated Management System (ISO 9001:2015, 14001:2015 &amp;45001:2018) and implications of not conforming with the requirements.
	Understand and identify hazards, risks, environmental aspect and Impact as it is related to their jobs.


Educational Qualification and Work Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or its equivalent in Accounting, Finance, Economics or any other related discipline.
	Three (3) to seven (7) years of experience with at least two (2) years in Budget and Planning or similar field.
	Candidates must possess an ACA qualification. Additional certifications such as CIMA, CPA, or ACCA are an added advantage.


Skills and Competencies:


	Good understanding of trends, challenges, opportunities, regulations and legislations relating to the power sector.
	Good understanding of micro and macroeconomic indices and their impact on the business.
	Advance financial and accounting knowledge, including understanding of IFRS, finance best practices and other applicable reporting and accounting standards.
	Working knowledge of accounting packages and systems, including a standard ERP software.
	Very sound business acumen &ndash; financial and commercial knowledge.
	Good report writing and presentation skills.
	Ability to extract, manipulate and analyze large volumes of data and strong problem-solving skills
	Strong interpersonal, communication and negotiation skills
	High sense of responsibility, accountability and dependability
	Good oral and written communication skills&nbsp;
	Good problem-solving skills.
	High ethical standards and integrity.
	Proficiency in the use of MS Office tools.

Budget & Financial Analyst Job at Egbin Power Plc




        
    

        
        Egbin Power Plc is one of the biggest additions to the electricity industry in Nigeria. Often seen as the biggest single generating power station in Sub-Saharan Africa, it was built to meet the ever-rising demand for electricity in Lagos and its environs.

Following the nation’s privatization exercise, Sahara, through its power division, Sahara Power Group and sundry affiliations, acquired the 1320 MW installed capacity Egbin Power Plant. We are working towards deploying a minimum of 5,000 MW of electricity generation over the next five years.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Budget & Financial Analyst
     

            
    



Job Identification: 544
Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria.
Job Schedule: Full time
Job Category: Non-Technical

Job Description


	The Budget & Financial Analyst assists in managing Egbin’s budget preparation and monitoring process, including the preparation of relevant reports to support Management’s decision-making.


Key Duties and Responsibilities


	Assist in circulating the annual business planning guidelines and provide assistance to other budget holders during the planning process in conjunction with the performance and planning unit.
	Develop a consolidated budget pack for review and approval by the Management Account & Budget Manager.
	Monitor approved budget allocations, revenue, and expenditure and highlight variances for the attention of the Head, Management Account & Budget.
	Carry out primary review and validation of budget data presented by various units and or highlight major inconsistencies/shortcomings.
	Carry out initial review & analysis for consistency of data, validation with other operational information and present to the head of the section.
	Ensure the reporting of the monthly financial performance of each unit and business generating centres (BGCs)
	Carry out variance analysis of major performance parameters with that of budget forecasts, and highlight underlying business/operational causes of variation 
	Carry out studies and research of relevant authoritative sources of macroeconomic data to gather basic information on budgetary assumptions.
	Prepare first drafts of relevant periodic and/ or ad hoc reports.
	Assist in generating monthly management accounts, business performance statements, noting major deviations from budget, and risk areas.
	Participate in the analysis of operational results and proffer recommendations to management.
	Obtain key insights on industry (competitors) and economic analysis, and recommend areas of improvement (If any).
	Provide analytic insight using trend analysis on key operational performance and accounting indices.
	Prepares draft financial forecast to aid business decision-making.
	Prepare monthly financial investment analysis and suggest best alternatives for fund based on economic performance.
	Perform any other duties as may be assigned by the Head of Budget, Planning and Business Performance.
	Implement data protection policies and practices of Egbin Power Plc.
	Ensure compliance with the NDPR and other data protection laws, and data protection policies.
	Undertake Data Protection Impact Assessment as applicable to curb the risk during data processing operations.
	Ensure awareness, understanding and application of QHSE policy and application of departmental objectives.
	Ensure awareness of the Integrated Management System (ISO 9001:2015, 14001:2015 &45001:2018) and implications of not conforming with the requirements.
	Understand and identify hazards, risks, environmental aspect and Impact as it is related to their jobs.


Educational Qualification and Work Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree or its equivalent in Accounting, Finance, Economics or any other related discipline.
	Three (3) to seven (7) years of experience with at least two (2) years in Budget and Planning or similar field.
	Candidates must possess an ACA qualification. Additional certifications such as CIMA, CPA, or ACCA are an added advantage.


Skills and Competencies:


	Good understanding of trends, challenges, opportunities, regulations and legislations relating to the power sector.
	Good understanding of micro and macroeconomic indices and their impact on the business.
	Advance financial and accounting knowledge, including understanding of IFRS, finance best practices and other applicable reporting and accounting standards.
	Working knowledge of accounting packages and systems, including a standard ERP software.
	Very sound business acumen – financial and commercial knowledge.
	Good report writing and presentation skills.
	Ability to extract, manipulate and analyze large volumes of data and strong problem-solving skills
	Strong interpersonal, communication and negotiation skills
	High sense of responsibility, accountability and dependability
	Good oral and written communication skills 
	Good problem-solving skills.
	High ethical standards and integrity.
	Proficiency in the use of MS Office tools.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Social Media Manager Job at Pro Technology Limited




        
    

        
        Pro Technology Limited is a dynamic and rapidly growing company charged with the supply of brand new office equipment’s dedicated to delivering cost effective and efficient equipment. We pride ourselves on fostering a collaborative and innovative work environment where individuals can thrive and contribute to our shared success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Manage the company's social media accounts across various platforms, including Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, and X.
	Manage the company's online marketplace accounts, including Jiji, ensuring products are accurately listed and updated.
	Respond promptly to customer enquiries received through social media, Jiji, and other online channels.
	Develop and implement social media and online marketing strategies aligned with business objectives.
	Create, schedule, and publish engaging content, including text, graphics, and videos.
	Plan, execute, and optimise paid advertising campaigns across relevant digital platforms.
	Monitor social media performance, website traffic, online marketplace activity, and campaign analytics, providing regular reports and recommendations.
	Stay updated on industry trends, emerging platforms, and best practices in social media and online marketing.
	Collaborate with the team to promote products, services, and brand initiatives.


Requirements


	Minimum of 1 year of experience in social media management or digital marketing with demonstrable results.
	Strong understanding of social media platforms, content creation, and audience engagement strategies.
	Experience running paid advertising campaigns is required.
	Proficiency in social media management, analytics, and scheduling tools.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Strong organisational skills and attention to detail.
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
